zh.lifeSummary: 當Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland於1200年1月出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 維其可洛福阿可洛福 卡素時，他的父親Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster35歲，母親Maud Le Vavasour23歲。他於大約1222年與Joan De Marisco在愛爾蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1230年7月19日在法國波特-哈倫斯維安波爾斯享年30歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 維其可洛福阿可洛福 卡素。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland于1200年1月出生在爱尔兰庫迪 維其可洛福阿可洛福 卡素时，他的父亲Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster35岁，母亲Maud Le Vavasour23岁。他于大约1222年与Joan De Marisco在爱尔兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1230年7月19日在法国波特-哈倫斯維安波爾斯享年30岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 維其可洛福阿可洛福 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Theobald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from þeud ‘people, race’ + bald ‘bold, brave’. The first element was altered under the influence of Greek theos ‘god’. This name was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain.

en.lifeSummary: When Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland was born in January 1200, in Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Ireland, his father, Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster, was 35 and his mother, Maud Le Vavasour, was 23. He married Joan De Marisco about 1222, in Ireland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 19 July 1230, in Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, France, at the age of 30, and was buried in Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland est né en janvier 1200, à Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irlande, son père, Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Maud Le Vavasour, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Joan De Marisco environ 1222, à Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 19 juillet 1230, à Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, France, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterré à Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irlande.

de.Hazlewood With Storiths, Yorkshire, Engl: Hazlewood with Storiths, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland im Januar 1200 in Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster 35 und seine Mutter Maud Le Vavasour 23. Er hat um 1222 in Irland Joan De Marisco geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 19. Juli 1230 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, Frankreich und wurde in Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland è nato il nel gennaio 1200 nel Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irlanda, il suo padre, Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Maud Le Vavasour, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Joan De Marisco circa 1222, nel Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 19 luglio 1230, nel Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, all'età di 30 anni, e fu sepolto a Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Irelandが1200年1月にアイルランドコウントイ ウィックロウアルクロウ カストルで生まれた時，父Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancasterは35歳，母Maud Le Vavasourは23歳であった。約1222年にアイルランドでJoan De Mariscoと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1230年7月19日にフランスポイトウ-チャーエンツヴィエンポイティエルズで30歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ウィックロウアルクロウ カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.Hazlewood With Storiths, Yorkshire, Engl: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 하즐루드 위스 스토리스
ko.lifeSummary: Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland(이)가 1200년 1월 아일랜드 카운티 윅로 아클로 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Le Vavasour(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1222년 아일랜드에서 Joan De Marisco(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1230년 7월 19일 프랑스 포이토우-처츠 비니 포이티어스에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 윅로 아클로 카스틀에 묻혔다.

pt.Hazlewood With Storiths, Yorkshire, Engl: Hazlewood with Storiths, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland nasceu em janeiro de 1200, em Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irlanda, seu pai, Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Maud Le Vavasour, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan De Marisco aproximadamente 1222, em Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 19 de julho de 1230, em Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, França, com 30 anos, e foi sepultado em Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irlanda.

ru.Hazlewood With Storiths, Yorkshire, Engl: хазльвуд вит сторитс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland родился январь 1200 г. в местности арклоу кастл, коунти викклоу, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Le Vavasour, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan De Marisco около 1222 г. в местности Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 19 июля 1230 г. в местности поитиирс, виенн, поитоу-чарентесь, Франция в возрасте 30 лет и был похоронен в местности арклоу кастл, коунти викклоу, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Theobald Le Boteler 2nd Chief Butler of Ireland nació en enero de 1200, en Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irlanda, su papá, Theobald Fitzwalter Le Botler 1st Baron Butler Of Ireland And England Sheriff Of Lancaster, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Maud Le Vavasour, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Joan De Marisco aproximadamente 1222, en Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 19 de julio de 1230, en Poitiers, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, a la edad de 30 años, y fue sepultado en Arklow Castle, County Wicklow, Irlanda.
